description: v1 zero leg-return predictor (pipeline sanity)
notes: All predictions 0; scoring emits NaN Sharpe because Spearman over constant input is undefined. Confirms pipeline wiring (leg composition -> 424 target cols) works end-to-end. Verified lag convention: label[t, lag=L] = log(p[t+1+L]/p[t+1]).

description: v2 ridge leg model, minimal features (ret1/5/20, alpha=10)
notes: First ridge baseline. Overall close to zero; fold 3 clearly better (+0.159) vs folds 0-2 negative. Suggests per-asset OLS on short-window returns alone is barely above noise but the signal exists in recent regime. Non-degenerate predictions (range -0.62..+0.51, mean |p|≈0.015).

description: v3 ridge leg model, extended features (ret1/5/20/60+vol+mom+rank+beta+dow, alpha=10)
notes: Extended features improve overall Sharpe vs v2 (-0.0153 -> +0.0254). Folds 1-3 positive, fold 0 slightly negative. Last-fold-delta narrowed from +0.234 to +0.084.

description: v4 direct per-target ridge, same extended features (alpha=10)
notes: Direct per-target ridge ties leg composition (+0.0267 vs +0.0254). Structural hypothesis NOT confirmed so far — leg composition gives no edge at alpha=10. Direct runs ~2x faster (no leg composition). Next: tune alpha for both.

description: sweep alpha=0.1 (leg ridge, extended feats)
notes: alpha too low -> overfit.

description: sweep alpha=1 (leg ridge, extended feats)
notes: still under-regularized.

description: sweep alpha=100 (leg ridge, extended feats)
notes: Big jump vs alpha=10 (+0.055 vs +0.025). Fold 0 still negative.

description: sweep alpha=1000 (leg ridge, extended feats)
notes: Best so far. Almost tied with alpha=100. alpha=[100,1000] is the sweet spot.

description: sweep alpha=10000 (leg ridge, extended feats)
notes: Over-regularized; signal muted.

description: v5 wide features, all price-like cols, ret1/5/20 + leg extras (alpha=100)
notes: Best leg ridge so far. Wider feature set with non-leg price-like cols adds value over v3. Fold 0 still negative.

description: v5 alpha=50 (wide features)
notes: Near plateau: alpha in [50,100] all give ~0.064-0.065.

description: v5 alpha=500 (original submission)
notes: Baseline with wide features at alpha=500.

description: v7 ensemble leg+direct ridge, wide feats (alpha_leg=100 alpha_dir=10)
notes: Huge jump: 0.065 -> 0.089. Fold 0 turns POSITIVE for first time. Leg composition + direct per-target decorrelate well. Per-column z-score normalization before averaging removes target-level bias important for cross-sectional Spearman.

description: v8 per-date rank ensemble leg+direct
notes: Rank averaging UNDERPERFORMS z-score averaging (0.064 vs 0.089). Rank discards confidence; z-score keeps it.

description: v9 ensemble weight sweep over {0.2..0.8}
notes: Weight plateau 0.0888-0.0895 across [0.2, 0.8]. Very flat. v7 config L=30 D=10 w=0.5 near optimum.

description: v10 3-way ensemble ridge_leg+ridge_direct+PLS_leg (n_comp=30)
notes: Adding PLS hurts: 0.089 -> 0.084. PLS too correlated with ridge_leg; adds noise. v7 2-way ensemble remains best. eval time 6x slower due to PLS.

description: v11 ensemble with wide_plus features (ret1/2/3/5/10/20/60 + sr + more xrank + more factor)
notes: Best v11 config (alpha_leg=1000, alpha_dir=100): 0.074, worse than v7's 0.089. More features = more noise here. Wide_plus does NOT help.

description: v6 xgboost leg model, wide feats, depth=4 eta=0.05 rounds=100
notes: XGBoost alone is weaker overall than ridge ensemble (0.050 vs 0.089) but FOLD 3 = +0.196 is best of any model. Bias differs from ridge — promising ensemble partner.

description: v12 per-lag alpha ridge ensemble
notes: Per-lag alphas = tied with v7 (0.089). No material gain from separate alpha per lag.

description: v13 bagged ridge (5 bags, feat_frac=0.6)
notes: Feature bagging HURTS (0.082 vs 0.089). Drop useful signal.

description: v14 v7 with per-label std normalization
notes: Label standardization ~ tied with v7 (0.0894 vs 0.0891). Fold 0 now POSITIVE (+0.021) vs v7 (-0.005).

description: v15 3-way ensemble ridge_leg + ridge_direct + xgb_leg (80 rounds, equal z-score weights)
notes: XGBoost adds +fold-3 (0.205 vs 0.173 for v7) but hurts fold 1 (0.046 vs 0.078). Net tied with v7. Scored offline by reading per-fold runs/ files because the validate.py process orphaned during pueue wait.

description: v16 v7 with per-date cross-sectional demeaning of leg labels
notes: Demeaning flattens fold distribution (fold 0: -0.005 -> +0.021). Net tied with v7.

description: v18 v7 + regularized xgb (depth=3, lambda=100, 50 rounds, weight 0.2)
notes: Regularized XGBoost still hurts ensemble vs pure ridge. XGBoost-based approaches don't beat ridge plateau.

description: v19 4-way ridge ensemble (leg_raw+leg_demean+direct_raw+direct_demean)
notes: Final submission. 4-way ridge ensemble. Tied with v7 (0.0891) and v14/v16 variants. Fold 0 clearly best across all ridge variants (0.0215). Plateau ~0.089-0.090. XGBoost ensembles didn't break past. Fast (<20s wallclock, CPU-only).

description: v20 direct-only ridge alpha sweep (wide features)
notes: Direct ridge ALONE peaks at alpha=100 (0.068). Confirms v7/v19 ensembling gain is real. Alpha sweep: a=3: 0.059, a=10: 0.062, a=30: 0.066, a=100: 0.068, a=300: 0.063, a=1000: 0.053.

description: v21 v19 + pair-spread features in ALL ridges
notes: Pair features in both leg and direct ridges: HURTS (0.075 < 0.089). Pair features confuse leg ridge (which predicts individual asset returns, not pair-relative).

description: v21 at alpha_leg=100, alpha_direct=30
notes: Pair features in all ridges + higher alpha = tied with v19. Fold 3 much better (+0.195); fold 0 worse.

description: v22 pair-spread features ONLY in direct ridge, leg ridge keeps wide
notes: BREAKTHROUGH: +0.005 over v19. Pair features targeted at direct ridge only. Big fold-3 gain (+0.039), no hit to other folds.

description: v22 alpha sweep
v22 with expanded pair features (dret1/2/3/5/20 + sprz20/60 + vol20) alpha_L30_D100
notes: Expanded pair features fill in the dret2/dret3 gap matching lag=2/3 labels and add pair-level volatility. Same overall as v22 but better fold distribution (fold 0: +0.02, fold 1: +0.09).

description: v22 + XGBoost direct (depth=3, 60 rounds)
notes: BREAKTHROUGH past 0.10! Adding XGBoost fit DIRECTLY to targets (not leg-level) with pair features boosted +0.007 over v22. XGBoost captures non-linear interactions in pair-spread space. All 4 folds improved.

description: v24 XGB hparam sweep
notes: Best: d5r60l100 = 0.1024. d4r80l30 = 0.1021. Plateau ~0.1014-0.1024. Marginal XGB tuning.

description: v25 6-way: ridge(leg×2 + direct×2) + xgb(direct + leg) all with pair features
notes: BIG JUMP past 0.11! Adding XGBoost-leg (fitting leg returns with pair features as input) gives +0.009 over v24. Fold 2 and 3 got major boost. Pair features matter even when target is leg-level because they provide pair-relative context useful for multi-output boosting.

description: v26 7-way: ridge×4 + xgb(direct + leg_raw + leg_dm) more rounds
notes: +0.005 over v25. Fold 1 and 2 improved notably. 3rd XGB on demeaned leg returns diversifies further.

description: v27: v26 without leg ridges (direct×2 + xgb×3)
notes: Essentially tied with v26 (0.1163). Leg ridges contribute ~0 net. Fold 2 better (+0.154 vs 0.134), fold 3 slightly worse.

description: v28 8-way: ridge×4 + xgb×4 (incl xgb_direct_dm)
notes: Adding xgb_direct_dm (4th XGBoost member) gives marginal +0.0007 over v26. Plateau around 0.117.

description: v29 v28 with more XGB rounds (direct 120, leg 150)
notes: More rounds HURT slightly (0.117 -> 0.115). XGBoost at 80/100 rounds is near-optimal. Stick with v28 config.

description: v30 v28 + volume features in direct/xgb feature set
notes: BREAKTHROUGH: +0.004 over v28. Volume/open-interest features improve folds 0+1 significantly. Fold 0: 0.005 -> 0.019.

description: v31 v30 + pair rank features (cross-sectional pair z-score rank)
notes: Pair rank features NEUTRAL: 0.1208 vs v30's 0.1213. Redundant with pair sprz features.

description: v30 with alternative XGBoost seeds (101 direct, 202 leg)
notes: Seed 2 alone: 0.1210. Avg of seed1+seed2 offline: 0.1209. XGBoost is stable across seeds; bagging no help.

description: FINAL SUBMISSION: v30 8-way ensemble (ridge × 4 + xgb × 4) with wide + pair + volume features
notes: Final best submission. Combines: leg-level & direct-target ridges (with & without per-date demeaning) + XGBoost on each of those 4 label variants. Key insight stack: (1) ensemble leg+direct ridges (+0.065->+0.089), (2) pair-spread features in direct/xgb feature set only (+0.089->+0.095), (3) XGBoost-direct with pair features (+0.095->+0.101), (4) XGBoost-leg with pair features (+0.101->+0.111), (5) XGBoost on demeaned leg returns (+0.111->+0.117), (6) volume features (+0.117->+0.121).

description: v32 v30 + leg rank ridge (9-way ensemble)

params.new_member: ridge on cross-sectional rank-transformed leg returns

notes: 9-way ensemble with rank-leg ridge. Tied with v30 (0.1216 vs 0.1213) within noise. Rank transform does not add significantly.

description: v33 v32 + per-lag prediction scaling (0, 0.5, 1.3, 1.3)
notes: BEST FINAL. Zeroing out lag=1 predictions (which have per-target time-series corr ~0.011 = essentially noise) and downweighting lag=2 gives +0.004 Sharpe. Lag=1 contributes no signal to the cross-sectional ordering and hurts by adding random ranks. Fold 0 takes a small hit (0.017->0.004) but folds 1-2 improve substantially.

description: v33 with zero lag=1+2 scales (0, 0, 1, 1)
notes: MAJOR WIN: +0.007 over previous v33 (0.125 -> 0.132). Zeroing lag=1 AND lag=2 removes noise that dilutes the rank. Per-target analysis showed 94% of lag=1 and 82% of lag=2 have corr<0.05 with labels (near-random at daily scale). fold 0 goes to ~0 (since lag=1/2 was its only signal) but folds 1-3 all improve significantly.
